message:
feat(cli): policy-gated read/search output, semantic/hybrid search, asupersync webhook transport

Major update to the ft CLI encompassing three interrelated changes across
robot mode, human CLI, and the notification transport layer.

1. Policy enforcement on read and search operations:

   Add `authorize_read_or_search_policy()` and
   `record_read_search_policy_audit()` helpers that evaluate the policy
   engine before any text is returned from get-text or search commands.
   This applies uniformly to:
   - `ft get-text` (human CLI) — new policy check + audit trail
   - `ft search` (human CLI) — policy check before lexical/semantic query
   - `ft robot get-text` (single pane and batch modes) — per-pane policy
     authorization with denied panes receiving structured error responses
     while authorized panes proceed normally
   - `ft robot search` — policy check on the search query with pane-scoped
     capability resolution when a pane filter is provided

   All four surfaces now pass returned text through `redact_for_output()`
   to strip secrets, and search results are redacted via the new
   `redact_search_results_for_output()` helper which sanitizes content,
   snippets, and highlights.

   New helpers: `robot_policy_error_from_decision()` converts policy
   decisions into structured (code, message, hint) triples for robot
   envelope responses.

2. Semantic and hybrid search mode support:

   Remove the `ensure_mode_supported()` guard that previously rejected
   non-lexical search requests across robot mode and human CLI. Instead,
   map the requested mode to `frankenterm_core::search::SearchMode` and
   route through `storage.hybrid_search_with_results()` with RRF fusion
   (k=60, constant `CLI_HYBRID_RRF_K`) for semantic/hybrid modes.

   The `unified_search_mode_to_core_mode()` helper bridges the query
   contract's UnifiedSearchMode enum to the core search module's SearchMode.

   For multi-pane bookmark-filtered searches, semantic/hybrid results from
   each pane are merged with descending score sort (vs ascending for
   lexical BM25) to respect the different score semantics.

3. Asupersync webhook transport:

   Replace `ReqwestWebhookTransport` with `AsupersyncWebhookTransport`
   that uses `asupersync::http::h1::HttpClient` for webhook delivery.
   The transport manually serializes the JSON body, constructs headers
   including Content-Type, and maps the response status check from
   reqwest's typed status API to plain u16 range checks.

4. Distributed mode asupersync migration:

   Replace all `tokio::io::*` trait usage in distributed mode with
   `asupersync::io::*` equivalents. Add `distributed_read_line()` to
   replace `tokio::io::AsyncBufReadExt::read_line()` with a manual
   poll-based implementation using `asupersync::io::AsyncBufRead`.
   Add `distributed_tls_domain()` for extracting the TLS server name
   from connect addresses (handles IPv6 bracket notation).
   Replace `tokio::net::TcpListener/TcpStream` with asupersync equivalents.
   Replace `tokio_rustls::TlsAcceptor/TlsConnector` with asupersync wrappers.
   Replace `tokio::sync::{mpsc, watch}` with `runtime_compat::{mpsc, watch}`.

5. Human CLI get-text enhancements:

   Add `--tail <n>` argument to `ft get-text` (default 200 lines) with
   truncation applied identically to robot mode. Update help text example.

Tests:
- `cli_get_text_parses_tail`: verify --tail flag parsing
- `read_search_policy_default_allows_robot_search`: default config permits
- `read_search_policy_rule_can_deny_robot_search`: custom policy rule denies
- `redact_search_results_redacts_sensitive_fields`: verify content/snippet/
  highlight redaction with AWS keys, Stripe tokens, passwords

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
